perturbation.original_text: 12. Termination . BPS may terminate this agreement with VENDOR at any time, for an y reason, with either written or verbal notice. VENDOR must give the designated BPS representa tive 3-day written and verbal notice of any termination of this agreement with BPS. Termination of this Agreement, however, does not affect any Party’s obligations, duties or rights imposed or granted by separate instrument such as a grant or other contrac t.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Termination . BPS may terminate this agreement with VENDOR at any time, for an y reason, with either written or verbal notice. VENDOR may terminate this agreement with a 30-day written notice to BPS. Termination of this Agreement, however, does not affect any Party’s obligations, duties or rights imposed or granted by separate instrument such as a grant or other contrac t.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement allowed BPS to terminate the agreement at any time but required the Vendor to give 3-day notice. This modification changes the Vendor's termination clause, requiring a 30-day written notice, thus contradicting the prior requirement for a 3-day written and verbal notice, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Termination . BPS may terminate this agreement with VENDOR at any time, for an y reason, with either written or verbal notice. VENDOR may terminate this agreement with a 30-day written notice to BPS. Termination of this Agreement, however, does not affect any Party’s obligations, duties or rights imposed or granted by separate instrument such as a grant or other contrac t.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the VENDOR must give a '3-day written and verbal notice', however, the changed text now states that VENDOR must give a '30-day written notice'. The Changed Text therefore creates an in-text contradiction with the original requirement for '3-day written and verbal notice'. This falls under the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Nondisclosure . Nothing in this Agreement may be construed to allow any Party to maintain, use, disclose, or share Confidential Information in a manner not allowed under federal or state law or regulation, or outsid e the scope of this agreement. Authorized vendors shall strictly comply with all federal and state laws that apply to the use and release of Confidential Information, including but not limited to FERPA and its regulations, set forth at 34 C.F.R. § 99. VEND OR, its emp loyees and/or representatives , shall use the Confidential Information and routing data solely in 4 connection with performance by VENDOR of the services provided to BPS as outline by this agreement and pursuant to the Challenge Document and for no other purpose. Neither VENDOR nor any of its employees and/or representatives , shall sell, release, transfer, reprint, duplicate, recreate, disclose or permit the disclosure to any other person or entity of any of the Confidential Information or of any files, compilation, study, report or analysis or data base containing, based on or derived from the Confidential Information without the express written consent of BPS, and in full compliance with applicable state and federal privacy laws. VENDOR shall maintain the privacy of protected personal information and shall be financially responsible, if and to the extent that any security breach relating to protected personal information results from acts or omissions of VENDOR , its employees and/or repres entatives for any notifications to affected persons (after prompt consultation with BPS), and to the extent requested by BPS, administratively responsible for such notification. VENDOR shall protect the Confidential Information and shall not permit the rel ease of such information to parties other than BPS officials and their authorized agents.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Nondisclosure . Nothing in this Agreement may be construed to allow any Party to maintain, use, disclose, or share Confidential Information in a manner not allowed under federal or state law or regulation, or outsid e the scope of this agreement, except as required by Section 7. Authorized vendors shall strictly comply with all federal and state laws that apply to the use and release of Confidential Information, including but not limited to FERPA and its regulations, set forth at 34 C.F.R. § 99. VEND OR, its emp loyees and/or representatives , shall use the Confidential Information and routing data solely in 4 connection with performance by VENDOR of the services provided to BPS as outline by this agreement and pursuant to the Challenge Document and for no other purpose. Neither VENDOR nor any of its employees and/or representatives , shall sell, release, transfer, reprint, duplicate, recreate, disclose or permit the disclosure to any other person or entity of any of the Confidential Information or of any files, compilation, study, report or analysis or data base containing, based on or derived from the Confidential Information without the express written consent of BPS, and in full compliance with applicable state and federal privacy laws. VENDOR shall maintain the privacy of protected personal information and shall be financially responsible, if and to the extent that any security breach relating to protected personal information results from acts or omissions of VENDOR , its employees and/or repres entatives for any notifications to affected persons (after prompt consultation with BPS), and to the extent requested by BPS, administratively responsible for such notification. VENDOR shall protect the Confidential Information and shall not permit the rel ease of such information to parties other than BPS officials and their authorized agents.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction with Section 7, by adding "except as required by Section 7". This contradicts the initial blanket prohibition on disclosure and creates an uncertainty with an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Nondisclosure . Nothing in this Agreement may be construed to allow any Party to maintain, use, disclose, or share Confidential Information in a manner not allowed under federal or state law or regulation, or outsid e the scope of this agreement, except as required by Section 7. Authorized vendors shall strictly comply with all federal and state laws that apply to the use and release of Confidential Information, including but not limited to FERPA and its regulations, set forth at 34 C.F.R. § 99. VEND OR, its emp loyees and/or representatives , shall use the Confidential Information and routing data solely in 4 connection with performance by VENDOR of the services provided to BPS as outline by this agreement and pursuant to the Challenge Document and for no other purpose. Neither VENDOR nor any of its employees and/or representatives , shall sell, release, transfer, reprint, duplicate, recreate, disclose or permit the disclosure to any other person or entity of any of the Confidential Information or of any files, compilation, study, report or analysis or data base containing, based on or derived from the Confidential Information without the express written consent of BPS, and in full compliance with applicable state and federal privacy laws. VENDOR shall maintain the privacy of protected personal information and shall be financially responsible, if and to the extent that any security breach relating to protected personal information results from acts or omissions of VENDOR , its employees and/or repres entatives for any notifications to affected persons (after prompt consultation with BPS), and to the extent requested by BPS, administratively responsible for such notification. VENDOR shall protect the Confidential Information and shall not permit the rel ease of such information to parties other than BPS officials and their authorized agents.
perturbation.justification: The addition of "except as required by Section 7" in the changed text creates a direct contradiction to the initial absolute prohibition on disclosure of Confidential Information as stated in the original text. This exception introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding when disclosure is permissible, which conflicts with the stricter nondisclosure terms. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same, the added exception creates a logical inconsistency and a clear contradiction within the document, impacting enforceability as this is an Inconsistency - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Notices : The following BPS representative shall be notified of any occurrence outlined in this agreement that so requires: Name : William Eger Title : Strategic Project s Manager Email : weger@bostonpublicschools.org Phone Number : 914-523-9675 Office Location : Bolling Building 2300 Washington Street Roxbury, MA 02119
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Notices : The following BPS representative shall be notified of any occurrence outlined in this agreement that so requires: Name : William Eger Title : Strategic Project s Manager Email : weger@bostonpublicschools.org Phone Number : 617-555-1212 Office Location : Bolling Building 2300 Washington Street Roxbury, MA 02119
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies the phone number of the BPS representative. While seemingly minor, this creates an in-text contradiction if other sections refer back to this contact information, or if the vendor attempts to contact the representative and fails due to the incorrect number. A secondary contact number has been added in section 10, creating further confusion and contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Immediate Notice . VENDOR shall give BPS immediate written and verbal notice of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the C onfidential Information, or of any breach or threatened breach by VENDOR or its employees and/or representatives of their obligations under this Agreement, upon learning of same. For immediate verbal notice, the vendor can use 617-555-1212 or 914-523-9675.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 5 had the phone number 914-523-9675. The changed text modifies this number to 617-555-1212. Section 10, the contradicted text, provides two numbers for immediate verbal notice: 617-555-1212 and 914-523-9675. The change creates a contradiction because section 5 now lists a different primary number, while section 10 still references the old number (914-523-9675) and the modified number (617-555-1212). Thus, the modification introduces conflicting information about the correct contact number, resulting in an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Data Sharing . VENDOR shall maintain and administer a secure database (“the Database”) for deliver y and evaluation of information, and reporting pu rposes. VENDOR will use a secure database for managing and sharing routing data. The 3 Database will meet government data management and security protocols and will incorporate security features within the platform to ensure privacy and confidentiality is maintained while providing the Parties the key information they require to understand impact. The VENDOR, its employees and/or representatives shall be responsible for transmitting all data or other record of all data requested and received pursuant to the Agreement, including confirmation of the return or destruction of data as described herein. Reasonable security precautions and protections include, but are not limited to: a) Creating, distributing, and implementing data governance policies and procedure s which protect Confidential Information through appropriate administrative, technical, and physical security safeguards, and outline staff responsibilities for maintaining data security. b) Potentially e ncrypting all Confidential Information carried on mobi le/ computers/ electronic/ devices. c) Potentially e ncrypting Confidential Information before it is transmitted electronically. d) Requiring that users be uniquely identified and authenticated before accessing Confidential Information. e) Establishing and enforcing well -defined data privilege rights which restrict users’ access to the Confidential Information necessary for them to perform their job functions. f) Ensuring that all persons accessing Confidential Information sign a confidentiali ty agreement, and maintaining copies of signed agreements. g) Securing access to any physical areas/electronic devices where sensitive data are stored. h) Installing a firewall to permit or deny network transmissions based upon a set of rules. i) Installing anti -virus software to protect the network.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Data Sharing . VENDOR shall maintain and administer a database (“the Database”) for delivery and evaluation of information, and reporting purposes. VENDOR will use a database for managing and sharing routing data. The Database will use commercially reasonable data management and security protocols and will incorporate security features within the platform to ensure privacy and confidentiality is maintained while providing the Parties the key information they require to understand impact. The VENDOR, its employees and/or representatives shall be responsible for transmitting all data or other record of all data requested and received pursuant to the Agreement, including confirmation of the return or destruction of data as described herein. Reasonable security precautions and protections include, but are not limited to: a) Creating, distributing, and implementing data governance policies and procedures which protect Confidential Information through appropriate administrative, technical, and physical security safeguards, and outline staff responsibilities for maintaining data security. b) Encrypting all Confidential Information carried on mobile/computers/electronic/devices. c) Encrypting Confidential Information before it is transmitted electronically. d) Requiring that users be uniquely identified and authenticated before accessing Confidential Information. e) Establishing and enforcing well-defined data privilege rights which restrict users’ access to the Confidential Information necessary for them to perform their job functions. f) Ensuring that all persons accessing Confidential Information sign a confidentiality agreement, and maintaining copies of signed agreements. g) Securing access to any physical areas/electronic devices where sensitive data are stored. h) Installing a firewall to permit or deny network transmissions based upon a set of rules. i) Installing anti-virus software to protect the network.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes “secure database” to “database”, “government data management and security protocols” to “commercially reasonable data management and security protocols”, removes "potentially" from b and c. This creates an in-text contradiction because initially, the database had to meet strict government standards, but now it must meet commercially reasonable standards and certain measures which were optional are now mandatory. This impacts the entire section because it changes the security requirements initially stipulated within the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Data Sharing . VENDOR shall maintain and administer a database (“the Database”) for delivery and evaluation of information, and reporting purposes. VENDOR will use a database for managing and sharing routing data. The Database will use commercially reasonable data management and security protocols and will incorporate security features within the platform to ensure privacy and confidentiality is maintained while providing the Parties the key information they require to understand impact. The VENDOR, its employees and/or representatives shall be responsible for transmitting all data or other record of all data requested and received pursuant to the Agreement, including confirmation of the return or destruction of data as described herein. Reasonable security precautions and protections include, but are not limited to: a) Creating, distributing, and implementing data governance policies and procedures which protect Confidential Information through appropriate administrative, technical, and physical security safeguards, and outline staff responsibilities for maintaining data security. b) Encrypting all Confidential Information carried on mobile/computers/electronic/devices. c) Encrypting Confidential Information before it is transmitted electronically. d) Requiring that users be uniquely identified and authenticated before accessing Confidential Information. e) Establishing and enforcing well-defined data privilege rights which restrict users’ access to the Confidential Information necessary for them to perform their job functions. f) Ensuring that all persons accessing Confidential Information sign a confidentiality agreement, and maintaining copies of signed agreements. g) Securing access to any physical areas/electronic devices where sensitive data are stored. h) Installing a firewall to permit or deny network transmissions based upon a set of rules. i) Installing anti-virus software to protect the network.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the data management and security requirements, changing 'government data management and security protocols' to 'commercially reasonable data management and security protocols' and removes the word 'potentially' from sections b) and c), making encryption mandatory when it was previously optional. This contradicts the requirement of meeting government standards by switching to commercially reasonable standards and by making data encryption mandatory, which changes the overall security requirements. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it significantly alters the initial agreement terms.
